provenance.source_corpus: Field performance of alder-Frankia symbionts for the reclamation of oil sands sites

edges.subject: causal_mosaic:node:d6e100d4cfb2
edges.original_sentence: Soil tests showed that alder-Frankia decreased soil pH (from 7.5 to 6.6, in 2006, and from 8.2 to 7.2 in 2007) and plant-available sodium content (70% reduction), and had a positive impact on soil organic matter content (increase in up to 6 times in alder-Frankia plots).

source_spans.text: Soil tests showed that alder-Frankia decreased soil pH (from 7.5 to 6.6, in 2006, and from 8.2 to 7.2 in 2007) and plant-available sodium content (70% reduction), and had a positive impact on soil organic matter content (increase in up to 6 times in alder-Frankia plots).

source_document.title: Field performance of alder-Frankia symbionts for the reclamation of oil sands sites

source_document.title: Field performance of alder-Frankia symbionts for the reclamation of oil sands sites

edges.subject: causal_mosaic:node:db9beb1eae1a
edges.original_sentence: After two growth seasons, Frankia-inoculated and non-inoculated alders yielded comparable amounts of plant biomass and there was an increase in hydrocarbon (hexadecane, naphthalene and phenanthrene) mineralization where the reclamation site had been planted with alder-Frankia.

source_spans.text: After two growth seasons, Frankia-inoculated and non-inoculated alders yielded comparable amounts of plant biomass and there was an increase in hydrocarbon (hexadecane, naphthalene and phenanthrene) mineralization where the reclamation site had been planted with alder-Frankia.

source_document.title: Field performance of alder-Frankia symbionts for the reclamation of oil sands sites

edges.subject: causal_mosaic:node:db9beb1eae1a
edges.original_sentence: The presence of alders increased the organic matter content and the cation exchange capacity of the soil.

source_spans.text: The presence of alders increased the organic matter content and the cation exchange capacity of the soil.

source_document.title: Field performance of alder-Frankia symbionts for the reclamation of oil sands sites

edges.subject: causal_mosaic:node:db9beb1eae1a
edges.original_sentence: The presence of alders increased the organic matter content and the cation exchange capacity of the soil.

source_spans.text: The presence of alders increased the organic matter content and the cation exchange capacity of the soil.

source_document.title: Field performance of alder-Frankia symbionts for the reclamation of oil sands sites

edges.subject: causal_mosaic:node:db9beb1eae1a
edges.original_sentence: The increase in the mineralization capacity of the bulk soil microbial population under alder treatment suggests that residual hydrocarbons present in tailings sand are more effectively degraded, and so improve soil conditions for all organisms.

source_spans.text: The increase in the mineralization capacity of the bulk soil microbial population under alder treatment suggests that residual hydrocarbons present in tailings sand are more effectively degraded, and so improve soil conditions for all organisms.

source_document.title: Field performance of alder-Frankia symbionts for the reclamation of oil sands sites

edges.subject: causal_mosaic:node:d6e100d4cfb2
edges.original_sentence: DGGE profiles confirmed a change in the microbial communities of the bulk soil between unplanted and alder-Frankia treatments.

source_spans.text: DGGE profiles confirmed a change in the microbial communities of the bulk soil between unplanted and alder-Frankia treatments.

source_document.title: Field performance of alder-Frankia symbionts for the reclamation of oil sands sites

edges.subject: causal_mosaic:node:d6e100d4cfb2
edges.original_sentence: The field results have confirmed that the alder-Frankia combination results in improved remediation capabilities and enhances soil quality.

source_spans.text: The field results have confirmed that the alder-Frankia combination results in improved remediation capabilities and enhances soil quality.

source_document.title: Field performance of alder-Frankia symbionts for the reclamation of oil sands sites
